message:
Add OpenAI platform usage collection

Users who make OpenAI API calls outside of Codex CLI (custom scripts,
agents, third-party tools using their API keys) have no way to report
that usage today. This wires in platform.openai.com/usage via the
/v1/organization/usage/completions endpoint, gated on a new optional
OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY env var.

Requires an admin API key (sk-admin-...) because regular project keys
can't query org-level usage. Only completions usage is collected — that
covers chat completions and the Responses API, where ~all token volume
lives. Embeddings/moderations are separate endpoints and can be added
later if needed.

Tokens are split into inputTokens/outputTokens/cacheReadTokens to match
ccusage semantics (OpenAI's input_tokens field already includes cached,
so we subtract input_cached_tokens to get non-cached input). Source
tagged "openai-api" to distinguish from Codex and Claude data.

Documented the double-counting risk prominently: if Codex CLI is
authenticated with an API key rather than ChatGPT subscription auth, its
traffic already shows up in platform usage and enabling this alongside
Codex collection will double-count. README and .env.example both warn.

Tests cover the pure transformation (bucketsToDaily) with nine cases:
happy path, multi-model per day, cross-day sorting, zero-token skipping,
missing model fallback, empty inputs, missing results array, negative
clamp, and custom source tag. collectOpenAIUsage is exercised for the
no-key path; live API integration is left manual.

Authored by Claude Code (Opus 4.6) at Jesse Vincent's direction.

files.patch:
@@ -37,3 +37,13 @@ DEMO_VIDEO_URL=
 # This is shown on your profile page so others can see your setup.
 # We NEVER send prompts, responses, conversation history, file contents, or API keys.
 REPORT_MACHINE_CONFIG=true
+# OpenAI Admin API key (sk-admin-...) — enables collecting platform.openai.com
+# usage via the /v1/organization/usage endpoint. Create at:
+#   https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/admin-keys
+# This is a READ-only usage query; the key never appears in the usage report
+# we send to the server. Leave unset to skip OpenAI platform collection.
+# WARNING: If your Codex CLI is authenticated with an API key (not ChatGPT
+# subscription auth), its usage already shows up in platform.openai.com usage
+# and setting this will double-count those tokens. Only enable this if Codex
+# is using ChatGPT auth or if you don't use Codex CLI.
+# OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY=sk-admin-...

@@ -144,6 +144,21 @@ The client supports reporting from multiple machines under the same username. Ea
 
 Your [profile page](https://tokenmaxxing.odio.dev) shows how many machines you're reporting from.
 
+## OpenAI Platform Usage
+
+If you make OpenAI API calls directly (not through Codex CLI), you can pull token usage from `platform.openai.com/usage` and merge it into your reports. This covers any OpenAI API usage — your own scripts, agents, third-party tools authenticated with your API keys, etc.
+
+1. Create an admin API key at [platform.openai.com/settings/organization/admin-keys](https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/admin-keys) (must be an Organization Owner). Regular project keys won't work — the org usage endpoint requires an admin key.
+2. Add it to `.env`:
+   ```
+   OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY=sk-admin-...
+   ```
+3. Run `npm run report`. You'll see a new `OpenAI platform` line in the output.
+
+The client only *reads* usage data; it never sends your admin key anywhere except `api.openai.com`. Reports use the `/v1/organization/usage/completions` endpoint, which covers chat completions and the Responses API — essentially all OpenAI token volume for most users.
+
+> **⚠️ Don't double-count Codex CLI:** If your Codex CLI is authenticated with an OpenAI API key (rather than a ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription), its traffic already appears in platform usage. Enabling `OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY` alongside Codex collection will double-count those tokens. Leave `OPENAI_ADMIN_KEY` unset if Codex is on API-key auth.
+
 ## Profile Page
 
 Each user gets a shareable profile at `https://tokenmaxxing.odio.dev/user/YOUR_NAME` showing:
